title: Arlong’s Rule Tightens Around Nami
scene: On pages 12–15, the story moves through Arlong Park and Nami’s dangerous position among the fish-men. Usopp arrives ahead of the others and gets pulled into the fear surrounding Arlong’s domain.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: look closely. He might look a little like Arlong,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's a real one this time...

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Stand back! I can't run away, leaving behind a defenceless woman and child!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: If it's just one fishman, I'm sure I can manage somehow.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Finally gave up, did ya now?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: It's the opposite, I've res-olved to take you on!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I told you, don't harm the fishmen!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: you're that woman from earlier... Who're you!?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: And what about that fish- man!?

dialogue.speaker: Nojiko
dialogue.text: My name's nojiko. I grow oranges here.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Wait a sec, you're the one who knocked me out!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Hmph, and after I was going to save you too...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: The one did the saving was me! This is why outsiders are so much trouble.

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: But you're from Gosa Village, right?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: If so, then you should understand that attacking the fishmen,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: you of all people should be more than well aware...

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I know, but...! But they killed my dad!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I won't forgive them even if I die!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: They brought Arlong that huge monster I've never even seen before and... pillaged and destroyed my entire town...! They killed so many people!

summary: This part builds the Arlong Park conflict by showing how Nami’s choices look contradictory from outside while Arlong’s power keeps the island trapped.
